tests_written: 6 column-level + 1 combination + 1 not-null on thermal_event_rate + 1 accepted-values on climate_zone

root_cause.headline: OTA 2.1 soft-precondition profile over-warms cells below 14 F — a software regression, not hardware failure
root_cause.detail: All 312 Volt EV cold-climate thermal events in week 20 share a single signature: active_ota_version = '2.1', preconditioning_mode = 'soft', ambient temp below 14 F. The v2.1 update pushed two weeks ago introduced an aggressive soft-precondition profile that over-warms battery cells when ambient temperature drops below 14 F. Warm-climate markets are unaffected because ambient temps never trigger the threshold. The regression is in the soft-precondition algorithm — an OTA rollback resolves the issue; no recall is warranted.
root_cause.affected_cohort: Volt EV cold-climate fleet with OTA v2.1, ambient temp below 14 F

without_wizard.label: Without dbt-wizard
without_wizard.summary: Question enters the analytics backlog. Reliability review runs without the root-cause answer.
narrative: VP Connected Vehicle Ops files the request. It enters the analytics backlog at priority A.
Data engineer scopes the work Monday morning — three days after the reliability review.
Engineer writes SQL from scratch, joining four silver tables by hand.
Tests, YAML, and contract get bolted on at PR time.
Reliability team cannot isolate whether this is hardware or software before the review.
Warranty team escalates a potential recall — $9M exposure stays open.

with_wizard.label: With dbt-wizard
with_wizard.summary: Four sub-agents surface the upstreams, author the SQL, write the tests, and materialize the asset in 91 seconds.
narrative: Explorer runs status and search. Returns the four silver upstream candidates.
Summary runs describe and lineage. Confirms join path and names the new grain.
Worker runs dbt_show on a 21-day slice. Validates the proposed grain.
Worker authors fct_thermal_flags_by_powertrain_climate_week.sql with CTEs and final SELECT.
Verification writes the YAML — description, schema contract, 9 tests, ownership.
Worker materializes the table to Iceberg. Verification confirms downstream lineage.
VP presents root-cause finding: OTA rollback, not recall. $9M exposure closed.

hero.label: Root cause identified before the Reliability Review — OTA rollback, not a recall
hero.note: From VP question to materialized Iceberg gold table — versioned, tested, documented, lineage-tracked. The connected vehicle team now knows OTA v2.1's soft-precondition profile is the regression. An OTA rollback closes the incident. The $9M warranty-and-recall exposure is resolved without a single vehicle leaving the road.
